title: Wudu
summary: The correct method of purifying the body before prayer, Tawaf, and touching the Quran.
intro: Purity is a prerequisite for Salah, Tawaf, and touching the Quran, and Wudu is one of the main means of attaining it. The method of Wudu is the same for both men and women; only in a couple of matters is there a slight difference for women, which is mentioned separately below.
fardsIntro: For Wudu to be valid, the following four acts must be performed. If even one of them is omitted, the Wudu is not valid:
fards: Washing the entire face once (from the hairline on the forehead to below the chin, and from one ear to the other).
Washing both arms including the elbows.
Washing both feet including the ankles.
stepsHeading: How to Perform Wudu (Step by Step)

steps.title: Intention and Bismillah
steps.detail: Make the intention (niyyah) for purification in your heart, and begin Wudu by saying "Bismillah".

steps.title: Washing the hands
steps.detail: Wash both hands up to the wrists three times. Run water between the fingers (khilal). If wearing a ring, move it so that water reaches underneath.

steps.title: Rinsing the mouth
steps.detail: Take water in the right hand and rinse the mouth three times. If not fasting, gargle well to clean the mouth thoroughly. Using a miswak, where possible, is Sunnah.

steps.title: Sniffing water into the nose
steps.detail: Take water in the right hand and sniff it into the nose three times, then clean it out with the left hand. If not fasting, let the water reach the soft inner part of the nose.

steps.title: Washing the face
steps.detail: Wash the entire face three times — from the hairline on the forehead to below the chin, and from the lobe of one ear to the lobe of the other. If you have a beard, run wet fingers through it (khilal).

steps.title: Washing the arms up to the elbows
steps.detail: First wash the right arm from the fingertips up to and including the elbow three times, then wash the left arm the same way. Do not forget to run water between the fingers.

steps.title: Wiping the head (masah)
steps.detail: Wet both hands and wipe the entire head once. Move your palms from the front of the head to the back, then bring them forward again.

steps.title: Wiping the ears (masah)
steps.detail: With the same wet hands, wipe the inside of the ears with the index fingers and the outside of the ears with the thumbs.

steps.title: Washing the feet
steps.detail: First wash the right foot including the ankle three times, then wash the left foot. Run the little finger of the left hand between the toes.

dua.heading: Dua After Wudu
dua.context: The beloved Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Whoever among you completes Wudu perfectly and then recites (the following dhikr), all eight gates of Paradise are opened for him; he may enter through whichever gate he wishes."
dua.arabic: أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ وَأَشْهَدُ أَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا عَبْدُهُ وَرَسُولُهُ
dua.pronunciation: Ash-hadu al la ilaha illallahu wahdahu la sharika lah, wa ash-hadu anna Muhammadan 'abduhu wa rasuluh.
dua.translation: I bear witness that there is no true god but Allah, alone, without any partner, and I bear witness that Muhammad (ﷺ) is His servant and Messenger.
dua.reference: Sahih Muslim 234, Abu Dawud, Ibn Majah

extra.note: In the narration of Tirmidhi, the following part is also added at the end of this dua:
extra.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اجْعَلْنِي مِنَ التَّوَّابِينَ، وَاجْعَلْنِي مِنَ الْمُتَطَهِّرِينَ
extra.translation: O Allah! Make me among those who repent often, and make me among those who purify themselves.

sunnahs: Rinsing the mouth and sniffing water into the nose
Running fingers through the beard and between the fingers (khilal)
Following the correct order (tartib) of the limbs
Washing each limb before the previous one dries (doing it without pause)

womenNotes.heading: Special Points for Women
womenNotes.intro: The method of Wudu is the same for both women and men. However, women should keep a few points in mind:

items.title: Wiping the head
items.detail: If the hair is braided or tied up, there is no need to undo it; passing wet hands over the top of the head is sufficient.

items.title: Nail polish
items.detail: If there is nail polish or anything on the nails that prevents water from reaching them, it must be removed; otherwise the Wudu will not be valid. However, henna colour on the hands does not cause any problem.

items.title: Rings / bangles
items.detail: If water does not reach underneath a ring or bangle, it must be moved so that water can reach.

items.title: Makeup
items.detail: If there is a layer of makeup or cream on the face that prevents water from reaching the skin, it must be removed.

breakers.heading: What Invalidates Wudu
items: Blood, pus, or any flowing discharge coming out of the body
Falling asleep lying down or leaning to one side

title: Method of Salah
summary: The step-by-step way the five daily prayers are performed.
steps: Face the Qiblah and make the intention
Say the Takbeer al-Ihram ("Allahu Akbar") to begin
Recite the opening supplication, seeking refuge, and Bismillah
Recite Surah al-Fatihah and another surah
Rise from Ruku and stand straight
Perform Sujood (prostration) twice, sitting briefly in between
Repeat this sequence for each rak'ah
In the final sitting, recite Tashahhud, Durood, and supplications

title: Nullifiers of Salah
summary: Actions and situations that invalidate the prayer.
steps: Turning the chest away from the Qiblah
Eating or drinking during the prayer
Exposure of the parts of the body required to be covered (satr)
